'''Kolig''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] and criminal. Serving as the unofficial leader of the [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] hunter group known as [[Qulok's Fist]], he was also the [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] and owner of the organization's [[Starship/Legends|vessel]], the ''[[Steel Fist]]'', a heavily modified [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighter]]. Presentable and affable, Kolig took the lead in organizing operations, and often went undercover to investigate and appraise potential targets. While working in the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] around [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], Kolig and Qulok's Fist were employed by [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]], the local [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]]. They were paid to tail the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[FarStar]]'', which had been assigned to [[DarkStryder Campaign|track]] Sarne following the Moff's flight from the [[Sector/Legends|sector]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]] due to its [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|liberation]] by New Republic [[New Republic task force (Kal'Shebbol)|forces]]. Eventually, Kolig and the group [[Skirmish at Shintel|engaged]] the ''FarStar'' in the [[Shintel system/Legends|Shintel system]] at Sarne's request, damaging the [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]] before retreating.

==Biography==
Kolig, a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], was a member of the [[Qulok's Fist]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] group, a collection of individuals that included [[Thanis Gul-Rah]], [[Sufar]], and [[Padak]], who engaged in various illegal activities including [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] hunting, [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]], [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]], and [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] work. Serving as the unofficial leader of the gang, he was also the [[Brain/Legends|brains]] behind the outfit. Kolig was also the owner and [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] of the ''[[Steel Fist]]'', a modified [[Corellian Engineering Corporation/Legends|Corellian Engineering Corporation]] [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighter]] that served as Qulok's Fist's mobile base of operations. Kolig spent many [[Standard year/Legends|years]] modifying the [[Starship/Legends|ship]] to his own specifications, and took exception to anyone who damaged it.<ref name="tdc" />

Kolig's main role was to organize the group's operations, but he would often lend assistance to any ground-side mission. Considered to be the most outgoing member of Qulok's Fist, he often posed as a [[free-trader]] or freelance pilot to investigate targets that the group planned to hit. At some point, Kolig and Qulok's Fist rescued the [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] criminal Sufar from [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] bounty hunters. Sufar, who was being [[Hunting/Legends|hunted]] due to a string of [[Death/Legends|murders]] he had committed, joined their ranks, and the gang tracked down the hunters and allowed the Corellian to [[Torture/Legends|torture]] them to death. The group worked in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] during the early years of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], eventually passing through the [[Minos Cluster/Legends|Minos Cluster]] and ending up in the [[Kathol sector/Legends|Kathol sector]] some [[Time/Legends|time]] around [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]]. While in the Kathol [[Sector/Legends|sector]], the group also worked for the local Imperial [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]], [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Kentor Sarne]]. During that time, a hunt on a backwater [[Planet/Legends|planet]] turned into a massacre of a local settlement. That action prompted Thanis Gul-Rah to leave the organization, and settle on [[Kal'Shebbol/Legends|Kal'Shebbol]], the sector [[Capital/Legends|capital]].<ref name="tdc" />

In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]],<ref name="tdc" /> following the [[Battle of Kal'Shebbol|liberation]] of Kal'Shebbol by New Republic [[New Republic task force (Kal'Shebbol)|forces]], Moff Sarne was forced to flee deep into the Kathol sector.<ref name="tsb">{{DarkStryder|adventure=The Saga Begins|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}}</ref> After learning that a New Republic [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]], the ''[[FarStar]]'', had been ordered to [[DarkStryder Campaign|track him down]], Sarne employed the services of Kolig and Qulok's Fist to tail the [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]] and gather information on its movements for him.<ref name="tdc" />

[[File:Steel_Fist.jpg|thumb|left|The ''Steel Fist'', Kolig's personal ship]]
After Qulok's Fist had shadowed the ''FarStar'' for a while, Sarne contacted them and informed them of a plan he was putting into motion to delay the corvette in the [[Shintel system/Legends|Shintel system]].<ref name="sd">{{DarkStryder|adventure=Shintel Downtime|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}}</ref> Sarne had arranged for the ''FarStar'' to divert to the planet [[Shintel]] by using a deep cover agent on [[Gandle Ott]], [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Olaver Lansel]], to pass on information to the corvette's command crew. The information indicated that Sarne's destination upon leaving Gandle Ott was the Shintel system, and the crew duly followed the lead to pursue the rogue Moff.<ref name="dirp">{{DarkStryder|adventure=Death Is Remotely Possible|book=The DarkStryder Campaign}}</ref> At Shintel, the vessel discovered an [[Imperial Depot 4290-1|Imperial base]] that Sarne had abandoned since he believed the personnel stationed there were more loyal to the Empire than to Sarne himself. During the corvette's visit to Shintel, another agent, [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Palme]], managed to upload a [[Computer/Legends|computer]] virus into the ''FarStar''{{'s}} systems that caused the [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] to blow out when the crew attempted to engage it to leave the system. Kolig and Qulok's Fist were to journey to the [[Star system/Legends|system]] and [[Skirmish at Shintel|attack]] the corvette in a hit-and-fade operation. Arriving in-system, the gang found the corvette stranded, just as Sarne had planned. Kolig, piloting the ''Steel Fist'', conducted several fast attacks on the ''FarStar'' before withdrawing from the system as the ship began to recover. The corvette suffered minor damage,<ref name="sd" /> as Sarne had only intended to harass and inconvenience the vessel. The Moff wanted the ''FarStar'' to follow him, which in turn would draw other New Republic forces into a trap he was preparing so he could destroy them in one fell swoop.<ref name="ko">''[[The Kathol Outback]]''</ref>

==Personality and traits==
Kolig had dark skin and [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] that was kept neat up top and long at the back, with a long thin mustache that curled at the ends. Well-skilled in combat, Kolig was known to be ruthless. He carried two [[Heavy blaster pistol/Legends|heavy blaster pistols]] and possessed a grasp of combat tactics, knowing when to strike at a target and when to keep his distance. He also knew that he would have no chance to spend any [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] he earned if he wound up dead through an error. Kolig also particularly despised [[Alien/Legends|aliens]], believing that they should be enslaved. In his opinion, the more exotic the [[Species/Legends|species]], the more it deserved to be in captivity.<ref name="tdc" />

Presentable and dashing, Kolig was supremely calm and confident. He was a smooth operator, and was as at home infiltrating potential targets as he was in combat. A skilled pick pocket, Kolig was especially charming to the ladies, but could not stand rejection from them. Despite his calmness, small annoyances could set him off, including female rejection. He was also extremely proud and protective of the ''Steel Fist'', and despised anyone damaging or even marring the vessel in any way.<ref name="tdc" />

An accomplished pilot, Kolig could fly [[Realspace/Legends|space]] [[Transport/Legends|transports]]—with a particular expertise in vessels of the YT-1300 series—and [[Astrogation/Legends|navigate]] through [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. He could operate the [[Sensor/Legends|sensor]], weapons, and [[Deflector shield/Legends|shield]] systems aboard them, and was also an [[Engineer/Legends|engineer]], capable of repairing and modifying space transports and their weaponry. He was knowledgeable about planetary systems, and typically wore a pilot's [[Flak vest/Legends|flak jacket]], and carried a communications headset and [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]].<ref name="tdc" />

==Behind the scenes==
Kolig was created for ''[[The DarkStryder Campaign]]'', a supplement for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' by [[West End Games]] published in [[1995]]. Kolig appeared in the "Rogues Gallery" section written by [[Peter M. Schweighofer]], and was included so the Gamemaster could use him in an original adventure. Kolig would go on to appear indirectly in the adventure ''[[Shintel Downtime]]'', although he would only be encountered aboard his ship, the ''Steel Fist'', along with the rest of Qulok's Fist.
